statement: In early July 2026 market sentiment turned to doubt about the return on AI-infrastructure spending, with analysts warning that hyperscalers' 67% jump in AI capex to $650 billion 'may be unsustainable' — a demand-side risk for merchant AI GPUs even though no vendor shipment figures are given.
why: Hyperscaler capex is the primary demand engine for data-center GPUs; if that spending is judged unsustainable and its ROI is doubted, the GPU demand outlook weakens.

impact.mechanism: Investor doubt about AI-infrastructure ROI plus a possibly unsustainable 67% capex surge threatens the spending flow that drives merchant-GPU and HBM demand.

evidence.source: Forbes (Peter Cohan) — Inside the July 2026 semiconductor selloff
evidence.excerpt: doubt about the return on AI infrastructure spending, dot-com-level valuations and a more hawkish Fed

evidence.source: Forbes (Peter Cohan) — Inside the July 2026 semiconductor selloff
evidence.excerpt: hyperscalers' 67% jump in AI capital expenditures to $650 billion may be unsustainable.

confidence.basis: Stock-market sentiment and analyst commentary, not shipment or order data; secondary source.
